summary: Excellent soundboard capture of an upbeat 1984 show with Jerry on fire and Brent’s polished keyboards, highlighted by a standout “Feel Like a Stranger”.
review: The recording is a clean, remastered sound‑board (SB) that sounds pristine and tremendously clear. The performance shines – Jerry’s guitar is described as ‘on fire’, Brent weaves an elaborate tapestry on the keys, Phil drives the low end and Bill/Mick keep a solid groove, with Bob adding tasteful slides and vocal touches; the first set feels good and the second set ramps up to high‑energy Chuck Berry‑style closers, especially the praised “Feel Like a Stranger”.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both musical performance and soundboard quality across multiple detailed reviews; only one outlier negative comment does not outweigh the overall enthusiasm.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, polished, pristine remastered SB with crystal‑clear instrument separation and tremendous overall sound quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as good; second set deemed great with high‑energy Chuck Berry‑style closers, delivering an upbeat and flowing concert experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as ‘on fire’ with exceptional guitar work.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass thumping away underneath the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Chiming in at the right moments; noted improvement on slide playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard tickling the ivories and weaving an elaborate tapestry; praised as being in ‘prime time’.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill/Mick’s drums thumping away, providing a solid rhythmic foundation.
